growth_records.medium: MM-G minimal medium, glutamate as sole N source (Phase A)
growth_records.cite: Deantas-Jahn et al. Metabolic modeling of Halomonas campaniensis improves polyhydroxybutyrate production under nitrogen limitation. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1007/s00253-024-13111-8. [PMC11045607]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 (Glutamate medium, Phase A 0-30 h): Biomass growth 0.073 +/- 0.002 1/h; Glucose -0.782 +/- 0.25; Glutamate -1.169 +/- 0.014; NH4 0.123 +/- 0.153 mmol/g DCW/h. Negative = uptake, positive = production.
growth_records.notes: Reported specific growth rate mu = 0.073 h-1; doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.073 = 9.49 h (calculated). Rates from Table 1, batch experiments (biological duplicates). Sign convention (footnote): negative = uptake, positive = production; NH4 = +0.123 so it is a secretion here. 37 C, 200 rpm, pH adjusted to 9.0.

growth_records.medium: MM-G minimal medium, glutamate as sole N source (Phase B, N-depleted)
growth_records.cite: Deantas-Jahn et al. Metabolic modeling of Halomonas campaniensis improves polyhydroxybutyrate production under nitrogen limitation. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1007/s00253-024-13111-8. [PMC11045607]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 (Glutamate medium, Phase B 30-70 h): Biomass growth 0.042 +/- 0.006 1/h; Glucose -0.630 +/- 0.153; Glutamate Not applicable; NH4 -0.116 +/- 0.018 mmol/g DCW/h.
growth_records.notes: mu = 0.042 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.042 = 16.50 h (calculated). Rates from Table 1, Phase B. Glutamate flux 'Not applicable' in this phase (depleted). NH4 -0.116 = uptake. Batch, biological duplicates.

growth_records.medium: MM-G minimal medium, equimolar glutamate + NH4Cl N source (Phase A)

up.method: batch specific-rate fit (no net NH4 flux in Phase A)

growth_records.cite: Deantas-Jahn et al. Metabolic modeling of Halomonas campaniensis improves polyhydroxybutyrate production under nitrogen limitation. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1007/s00253-024-13111-8. [PMC11045607]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 (Glutamate and NH4 medium, Phase A 0-30 h): Biomass growth 0.062 +/- 0.089 1/h; Glucose -0.759 +/- 0.141; Glutamate -0.837 +/- 0.083; NH4 0.0 mmol/g DCW/h.
growth_records.notes: mu = 0.062 h-1 (SD 0.089 is large); doubling = ln2/0.062 = 11.18 h (calculated). Rates from Table 1. NH4 net flux 0.0 in Phase A (glutamate preferentially used). Equimolar glutamate+NH4Cl medium, total N 18.7 mmol/L. Batch, biological duplicates.

growth_records.medium: MM-G minimal medium, equimolar glutamate + NH4Cl N source (Phase B, N-depleted)
growth_records.cite: Deantas-Jahn et al. Metabolic modeling of Halomonas campaniensis improves polyhydroxybutyrate production under nitrogen limitation. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2024). DOI:10.1007/s00253-024-13111-8. [PMC11045607]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 (Glutamate and NH4 medium, Phase B 30-70 h): Biomass growth 0.039 +/- 0.006 1/h; Glucose -0.797 +/- 0.24; Glutamate Not applicable; NH4 -0.077 +/- 0.071 mmol/g DCW/h.
growth_records.notes: mu = 0.039 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.039 = 17.77 h (calculated). Rates from Table 1, Phase B. Glutamate 'Not applicable' (depleted); NH4 -0.077 = uptake. Batch, biological duplicates. PHB overproduction occurs under this N-limitation phase (not tabulated as a specific rate).
